title: MD
biography: Paul Farmer was an American physician and anthropologist who co-founded Partners In Health and transformed global health delivery in resource-poor settings. He pioneered community-based treatment for multidrug-resistant tuberculosis and HIV/AIDS in Haiti, Peru, Rwanda, and elsewhere, proving that high-quality healthcare is achievable in the world's poorest communities.
aiSummary: Paul Farmer was an infectious disease and global health specialist with an H-index of 95 at Harvard Medical School. Co-founded Partners In Health and revolutionized healthcare delivery in resource-poor settings. Based in Boston, United States.
techniquesInvented: Community-based directly observed therapy for MDR-TB

affiliations.role: Kolokotrones University Professor of Global Health and Social Medicine
Chief of Division of Global Health Equity

timeline.title: Co-founded Partners In Health in Haiti
timeline.description: Established PIH in Cange, Haiti to provide healthcare to the rural poor

timeline.title: Pioneered MDR-TB treatment in Peru
timeline.description: Proved that multidrug-resistant tuberculosis could be treated in resource-poor settings

timeline.title: Named UN Deputy Special Envoy to Haiti
timeline.description: Served as United Nations Deputy Special Envoy

timeline.title: Chair of Global Health and Social Medicine at Harvard
timeline.description: Appointed Kolokotrones University Professor, the highest honor at Harvard
